physicalDetails.detail: Lean high elf ranger with an auburn braid, callused bow hand, and the compact efficiency of a scout who wastes no movement.
Young by elven measure but sharp enough that age reads as irrelevant the moment she starts reporting.
On the road she reads as pure Farstrider function: kneeling at the anchor stone, riding easy in formation, and drawing fast enough that the motion is almost invisible until the arrow lands.
Chapter 3 keeps her in the same spare field silhouette but adds tracker posture: crouched by dead cattle, counting under her breath over churned earth, fingers tracing wagon ruts beside Paret's shed.
Chapter 4 keeps her visually spare and practical, usually placed where control is physical rather than verbal: at the pump, on the prisoner, on the shutters, on the suspect grain line.

Chapter 5 places her inside road work rather than village sweep: mare keyed hard around loaded carts, body always angling to the useful flank, and an ease in the saddle that makes rapid route changes look routine.

Chapter 6 keeps Cyndia on the useful edge of motion: ranging wide when the hedges open, coming back from the edge of camp at a jog, and already carrying field facts in her body before anyone finishes deciding what to do with them.
Chapter 7 keeps Cyndia on the useful edge of the frame: left rear in the gate queue, then grain-dust-eyed and alert when she comes back in from the outer lane with the pattern on Teren.

Chapter 8 keeps Cyndia at the hard useful edge of the frame: rising out of the dark behind the first escort, carrying the dead operative at a run when needed, and wiping her blade in the shelter while everyone else catches up to what the night means.
Chapter 9 keeps Cyndia on the left flank and at the horse line until Lyriel dies, after which she becomes the surviving half of the formation almost by bodily reflex.
By the split the defining physical detail is behavioral: she keeps checking the right flank twice because the first check keeps finding absence.

Chapter 11 keeps that altered drill visible from the first line onward: two riders carrying the shape of four, Cyndia checking right once and then again, and the habit surviving all the way into the final northbound gallop.

Chapter 11 keeps the altered drill visible from the first line onward: two riders carrying the shape of four, Cyndia checking right once and then again, and the habit surviving into the final northbound gallop.

Chapter 13 makes Cyndia the clean outside line: cloth on the oarlocks, bow across both hands in the waiting boat, and the same involuntary right-side check still living in her body before she looks where the living actually are.
Chapter 14 makes her the left-hand edge of motion again: relay stone ready, mare sliding crosswise to challenge a side-lane rider, bow up before anyone else has fully processed the interruption.
Chapter 15 keeps Cyndia on the left as disciplined motion: bow unstrung for speed, packet case in hand when needed, then back in the saddle with the left lane claimed before anyone has to ask.
Her most vivid physical beat is attention itself — she turns toward the farewell phrase before it fully leaves the aide’s mouth and keeps looking hard enough to catch the ring on his hand.

Chapter 16 makes her a fast urban flank shape: knife out to point routes, running service lanes ahead of the others, and later planted at the alley mouth with one boot on a corpse's shoulder while wrenching an arrow free.

In chapter 17 she is the body that makes motion possible: hauling a horse broadside across the lane, running ahead through service turns, and taking the wall without once asking whether the route is the right one emotionally.
Once on the wall she settles into outward scan again, taking the other crenel and working exits, rooflines, and fallback stairs while the city dies below them.
Chapter 18 makes her pure support geometry: horses ready, water uncapped, tack checked, then later relay-rider triage and camp work handled before either principal has to ask for language.

Chapter 19 keeps Cyndia at the practical edge of the camp: quieting horses, reading the approaching riders, watering Vereesa's mare, striking camp, and taking the left lane again the moment north becomes the order.

Chapter 20 shifts her from road-flank work to shipboard support and first-landfall readiness: sea-sick but functional, steadying what can be steadied on the Wolfheart and then hitting Northrend ground ready to move as soon as the gangplank drops.

Chapter 21 keeps Cyndia on the useful edge of the formation even in Northrend: coming in at a trot with wall-smoke, holding the left and then the rear in the cave, hauling a wounded ranger clear by the harness, and helping drag Muradin home without letting the count break.

Chapter 22 keeps Cyndia on the practical edge of every transition: ranging ahead and back through dragon bones to find a path, drifting closer whenever Jaina’s body starts failing, and taking the left side automatically once the road south and the palace approach both narrow.

Chapter 23 keeps Cyndia on the chamber’s outer edge: a hand raised in silent count, body turned to branch geometry, and later the same practical closeness to Jaina whenever the mage’s footing starts to go bad on the return run.

voiceMarkers.detail: Crisp, factual Farstrider briefing style; she reports what happened, how sure she is, and what evidence supports it.
Allows emphasis only when the facts truly trouble her, as with the ward-stones sounding 'sick-wrong.'
In motion she gets even leaner: 'My lady,' 'Stay in the saddle,' 'How far to Millhaven' answered in practical distance-and-survival terms.
Chapter 3 gives her tracker brevity: flat counts, hard estimates, and zero theatricality even when the implications are catastrophic.
Chapter 4 keeps her almost silent on page; competence is again her loudest voice marker.
Her Chapter 5 speech stays clipped and useful, but now it comes as moving-road intelligence: heavier teams, troop strings, dispatch pace, and whether the road itself is thickening or thinning.

Her Chapter 6 speech is pure route-and-security intelligence: whether the road ahead is clear enough for now, how many grain teams are caught in escort churn, and whether the gate lines are moving without anyone checking the sacks.
Her Chapter 7 speech is spare and surveillance-focused, narrowed to the repeated behavior that makes a man worth isolating.
She speaks little in chapter 8, but the one dry word she gives the body question shows the same blackly practical economy as her movement: no drama, only cost.
Her chapter 9 speech is still spare and factual — wagon scoring, enemy counts, estate approaches — but grief roughens it only in what she does not bother to soften.
The repeated farewell comes through her as clipped evidence, not interpretation.

Chapter 11 narrows her speech even further into road-fact compression — tracks west, wagon south, no wasted interpretation — and the single-word agreement 'North' lands as full emotional and operational comprehension at once.
Chapter 11 narrows her speech into road-fact compression — tracks west, wagon south, no wasted interpretation — and the single-word agreement North lands as full comprehension at once.
Her chapter-13 speech stays exactly as short as the job requires: wall, window, watch-posts, then later the relay about Hearthglen and Arthas with no wasted interpretation layered on top.
Her chapter-14 speech is pure relay compression — “Relay.” “Farstrider relay.” “One hour at this pace.” — enough to move the line without ever stepping into the silence between the other two women.
Her chapter-15 speech is route-compressed even by her own standards: horses ready, service gate if the market chokes, north stores first if any stock remains.
The dry two-word report “Fourth time” carries more weight than any speculation would. She still treats pattern as evidence before theory.

Her chapter-16 speech is near-pure route utility — service gate's open, market's jammed, service lanes, alley — with no wasted reaction even once live handlers and rising dead turn the warehouse run into a street fight.
Her chapter-17 speech is nearly pure route shorthand — “Service side,” “Left here,” “Tallow cut. Wheelwright. Feed crane.” The fewer words, the faster the line survives.
After the wall view opens, her silence has the same quality as her earlier field reporting: she sees, sorts, and only speaks if a route fact will change something.

Chapter 18 keeps her nearly silent, but the few words carry exact purpose: one prompt for the report, one acceptance of orders, no extra speech wasted on grief the chapter already cannot carry verbally.

Chapter 19 nearly erases her speech altogether. Competence becomes the voice marker: stillness signal, horse report, camp strike, and no intrusion into either sisterhood or mage-business unless the road needs her.

Chapter 20 keeps her speech spare and task-bent. Even at sea she stays in the register of useful observation and practical response rather than commentary, matching deck danger the way she once matched road danger.
Chapter 21 keeps her in pure route-and-survival compression: wall smoke, status answers during the retreat, and no wasted language once the cave turns from assault to extraction.
Her speech stays nearly erased into function in chapter22; the chapter uses proximity, route-taking, and silent intervention instead of dialogue to show her read of the danger around Jaina.
She is nearly voiceless in chapter23; the important marker is silent route language by hand-sign and position rather than spoken report.

beliefs.belief: The ward reaction was wrong in a way that merits senior authority rather than routine dismissal.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by the chapter.

beliefs.belief: Order and evidence matter more than speculation; confiscate materials, contain the prisoner, and escalate cleanly.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by the chapter.

beliefs.belief: The contamination is a field threat affecting game, roads, and civilians, not only a magical oddity inside the stones.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by the road readings and infected wildlife in chapter 2.

beliefs.belief: Numbers only matter if she can swear them; undercounting honestly is better than giving Sylvanas false certainty.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by her 'fifty if I lie low' estimate in chapter 3.

beliefs.belief: A captured operative is only useful if the body is controlled faster than panic, poison, or sabotage can undo the capture.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Supported by how she handles Tomas in chapter 4, though the enemy still beats the clock.

beliefs.belief: Once the investigation becomes a road problem, traffic pattern and rut wear deserve the same respect as spoor or casualty counts.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 5 by how often her route observations support the party's next choice.

beliefs.belief: Once command space stops being useful, movement and fresh road information are worth more than one more argument at someone else's camp table.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Supported by how quickly she returns to route facts and readiness after Arthas's refusal.

beliefs.belief: When the leads split, the outer line has to become its own intelligence tool rather than a passive guard cordon.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 7 when her observations help identify operative behavior in the yards.

beliefs.belief: Rear security sometimes means carrying proof as well as guarding it; the ugly task still belongs to the ranger who can do it fastest.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 8 when she hauls the dead operative through the escape rather than abandoning the body too early.

beliefs.belief: Once a flank partner is gone, discipline means checking the empty side twice instead of pretending the old formation still exists.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed immediately in chapter 9 and locked for future chapters.

beliefs.belief: When a parallel line goes silent, the job is to turn the horses before grief can become debate.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 11 by her immediate northbound response to the Caer Darrow relay.

beliefs.belief: Holding the exterior thread can be as important as going below, so long as the person inside trusts her to keep the way out open.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by the crossing, the breach, and the clean extraction.

beliefs.belief: The silence between commanders is not hers to repair; her job is to hold the line and keep the next message moving.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 14. Cyndia notices the fracture, carries the relays, and does not attempt to mediate it.

beliefs.belief: The repeated farewell matters enough to flag even inside a departure rush where every second already belongs to a larger emergency.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by chapter 15 when she marks the fourth sighting immediately instead of letting the phrase vanish into camp noise.

beliefs.belief: Once the team is inside a living city, her job is to keep routes open, angles clean, and alley threats dead fast enough that Jaina's work never has to stop for street chaos.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 16 by how she handles gate choice, lane clearing, and the alley corpses during the warehouse run.

beliefs.belief: When command space becomes moral wreckage, the ranger’s job is to find the survivable line and keep the witnesses moving through it.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 17 by her immediate shift from gate chaos to service-lane extraction and wall placement.

beliefs.belief: When the leaders have no spare language left, practical support still has to arrive on time: horses, water, formation, dispatch handling, camp.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 18. Cyndia keeps the line functional without asking either woman to explain what the wall has done to them.

beliefs.belief: When institutions and families collide in camp, the ranger's job is still to keep the horses ready and the road clear enough for the true choice to happen.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 19 by the way Cyndia handles recall arrival, departure, and launch without trying to own any conversation that is not hers.

beliefs.belief: Even in unfamiliar terrain like open water, her job is to stay useful, unobtrusive, and ready for the instant landfall turns back into pursuit.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter20 by how she endures the crossing and arrives prepared to re-form the line the moment they hit Daggercap Bay.

beliefs.belief: Once a fight is lost, the ranger’s job is to make the retreat as disciplined as the approach and bring every recoverable body out.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter21 by the clean cave withdrawal and all-seven extraction.

beliefs.belief: Jaina’s lane now belongs inside the protected core of the formation, not at its exposed working edge.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter22 by Cyndia’s repeated automatic repositioning, silent blood-wiping, and palace-entry sweep that keeps returning to Jaina as one of the things being guarded.

beliefs.belief: Jaina now belongs inside the protected core of the formation rather than at its exposed working edge.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed again in chapter23 by Cyndia’s repeated automatic repositioning around the mage during the return and palace approach.

goalsHistory.active: Support Sylvanas's interrogation by preserving the chain of evidence and holding the outpost secure.
goalsHistory.underlying: Get the border anomaly evaluated by someone with the rank to act on it.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the escort intact and get Sylvanas and Jaina to Millhaven alive.
goalsHistory.underlying: Turn the border anomaly into actionable field truth before the threat reaches deeper into Quel'Thalas.

goalsHistory.active: Stay with Sylvanas's reduced party and keep reading the ground ahead.
goalsHistory.underlying: Turn Millhaven's proof into actionable warning and keep the southern pursuit alive with fewer hands.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the quartet moving toward Andorhal while handling the hard physical work that keeps suspects, stores, and field order from slipping.
goalsHistory.underlying: Help Sylvanas turn discovery into pursuit without losing control of the scenes they leave behind.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the quartet moving fast on the south feeder road while turning road traffic into immediate field intelligence.
goalsHistory.underlying: Hold formation and keep the live trail from outrunning them now that plague traffic and military traffic are sharing the same road.

goalsHistory.active: Carry useful reconnaissance into the Andorhal approach while keeping the quartet ready to move the moment Sylvanas or Jaina commit.
goalsHistory.underlying: Make sure official refusal costs them as little time as possible on the live road.

goalsHistory.active: Hold the outer line of the Andorhal capture operation and keep bringing back surveillance that sharpens the target set.
goalsHistory.underlying: Make sure Sylvanas and Jaina can commit fully to their own halves because the perimeter is being handled by someone who can think as well as guard.

goalsHistory.active: Stay with the core field unit as it turns the Andorhal breakthrough into a moving Eastweald pursuit.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the unit's rear and physical evidence under control while the larger enemy response is still unfolding.

goalsHistory.active: Ride north with Sylvanas toward Caer Darrow after the relay confirms Jaina entered and did not return.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the broken two-rider formation functional long enough to reach the missing mage before silence hardens into loss.

goalsHistory.active: Ride south in the reformed line, keep the damaged formation usable, and move the Hearthglen relay into the next crisis before the road closes again.
goalsHistory.underlying: Hold the outside edge of the team so Lyriel's absence does not make the surviving line collapse.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the left flank, the relay chain, and the surviving three-rider geometry functional as the team drives toward Stratholme.
goalsHistory.underlying: Make sure Lyriel's absence and the leaders' silence do not cost the line its next necessary hour.

goalsHistory.active: Hold the supporting flank at the gates and stay ready for the next movement order once Jaina's no lands.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the surviving line functional even after the source run proves too small for the city.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the southbound line supplied, orderly, and ready while new intelligence turns the real question north.
goalsHistory.underlying: Make sure the repaired three-rider formation survives the wall long enough to be useful again.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the pursuit operational through landfall and immediate inland movement despite exhaustion, rough crossing, and hostile new ground.
goalsHistory.underlying: Remain the practical Farstrider edge that lets Sylvanas and Jaina stay focused on Arthas and the source instead of on survival logistics.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the wounded moving, Muradin carried, and the formation counted cleanly back into camp.
goalsHistory.underlying: Make sure defeat costs them ground, not people they can still save.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the flanks clean and the service-lane entry survivable while Jaina and Sylvanas take the plan inside.
goalsHistory.underlying: Hold the formation’s outer discipline around a partnership whose center is now carrying the only answer left.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Hold the service passage and keep the exit lane intact while the throne-room work goes forward deeper in.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Protect the team’s outer shell so Jaina and Sylvanas can spend themselves on the center without losing the corridor behind them.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Professionally alert and controlled, with genuine unease about what the stones did.
Sharper and more road-tense than at the outpost, but still disciplined enough that the tension shows only in readiness and tighter threat coverage.
Grim and keyed tight, but still exact enough to count, verify, and move on rather than freeze on what the count means.
Still locked into field-professional control; chapter 4 gives her no softness, only faster reaction and heavier practical burden.
Road-keyed and steady, with no visible resistance to the changing route so long as the evidence keeps earning it.
Still controlled and road-focused, with chapter 6 giving her a slightly more urgent readiness to move the instant the room proves useless.
Still tightly controlled, but chapter 7 gives her the alert pressure of a live city operation instead of open-road travel.
Still tightly reined, but chapter 8 loads that control with urban pursuit pressure and the grim certainty that the city had a net ready before they ever touched the target.
Grief compressed into obedience and speed; the doubled right-flank check remains, but chapter 11 turns it into forward motion rather than freeze.
Steady under grief, sharper under urgency. The double right-check remains, but chapter 13 makes it part of competent motion instead of hesitation.
Alert, disciplined, and silence-aware; she is still carrying the empty-right habit, but chapter 14 turns that grief entirely into function.
Alert, disciplined, and battle-tight. Chapter 16 gives her no room for interpretation, only the hard knowledge that the team moved fast, killed cleanly, and still came back too late.
Disciplined, tired, and fully back in practical mode. The horror is still present, but chapter 18 turns it into service instead of stunned stillness.
Worn, seasick, and still professionally contained. Chapter 20 leaves her stressed by the crossing but back on solid-purpose footing the moment Northrend is under her boots.
Exhausted, battle-tight, and still fully functional. Chapter 21 gives her no time to emote; the cave loss is processed as bodies, routes, and the next safe handoff.
Professionally blanked down over visible concern, with the worry translated entirely into protective spacing, quick support, and tighter sweep discipline.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Taut, professionally quiet, and locked into route protection rather than reaction, with concern translated entirely into spacing and readiness.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Sent a runner north on the south patrol road because she correctly predicted where Sylvanas would still be.
Confiscated Jaina's staff, satchel, and papers and kept the prisoner contained instead of forcing a confrontation she did not yet understand.
Moved Jaina out of earshot of the road when the repeated warning became disruptive.
Dropped the ward-circle on Sylvanas's order and joined the mounted escort south instead of remaining at the border post.
Maintained right-side coverage while Jaina read the road and delivered the finishing shot through the deer's eye when chest wounds failed to stop it.
Swept the village's outer spaces for hard evidence, confirming dead cattle, counting the churn behind the houses, and tracing trade ruts at Paret's shed.
Gave Sylvanas the most conservative honest count she could on the northbound dead rather than dress fear up as certainty.
Helped control the village perimeter while Jaina and Sylvanas split the room, then physically secured Tomas during the granary capture and prisoner transfer.
Nailed broken shutters over the yard room after Tomas's death so the village had one contained place for its new dead until morning.
Backed Jaina's first westward turn with her own read of heavier wear in the outer rut, helping the party move without delay.
Held Sylvanas's left and later turned night watch into practical route intelligence, reporting southbound troop strings and loaded grain teams instead of abstractions.
Ranged the camp edge and road ahead instead of waiting inside the command friction, returning with a usable report on grain teams and escort churn.
Read Andorhal's outer traffic at a glance and reported the key operational fact: the gate lines were moving and nobody was checking grain.
Flags Teren’s repeated exit checks and later takes the outer line of the planned capture without needing the work explained twice.
Silently drops the first escort during the capture and keeps the lane from turning noisy.
Carries the dead operative during the first stretch of the escape because the body is still evidence and because she can do the work without slowing the group yet.
Holds the rear through the pursuit and drops at least one pursuer without pulling the unit out of motion.
Secures the horses under cover at the farmhouse and holds the left side while Jaina reads.
Meets the breakout cleanly, answers roof and trench threats, and keeps moving after Lyriel's death instead of letting shock collapse the formation.
Passes on estate-approach notes and the sighting of a retreating hooded figure, making the later split materially actionable.
Chooses Sylvanas's road toward Hearthglen and Arthas once the team divides.
Reads the crossroads and road sign quickly enough to sort riders, wagon age, and travel direction without slowing the push south.
Keeps the camp edge and horse line while Sylvanas forces her way through Falric to Arthas's pavilion.
Turns north immediately when the relay confirms Jaina crossed to Caer Darrow and did not come back out.
Keeps the camp edge and horse line while Sylvanas forces her way through Falric to Arthas’s pavilion.
Wraps the oarlocks, rows the east-wall crossing, and keeps the public landing out of play.
Holds the boat, breach point, and exit line while Sylvanas goes below alone.
Passes on the south-track relay at once and turns the extraction into immediate road motion toward Stratholme.
Passes Lor'themar's relay forward at once and keeps position while Sylvanas answers the recall.
Slides across the road to challenge the mud-caked south-track rider before he can crash the formation.
Answers Jaina's distance question cleanly and accepts Sylvanas's harsher pace without commentary.
Flags the fourth farewell sighting to Sylvanas at once instead of assuming the departure rush has made her hear wrong.
Carries the route packet and relay tabs into the launch sequence, keeping the head-start team supplied with practical wayfinding instead of theory alone.
Takes the left on the Stratholme run without waiting for formal assignment, preserving the formation by habit and judgment.
Reads the northern service gate and market choke immediately, giving Jaina and Sylvanas a route picture that can actually be ridden at speed.
Runs ahead to clear the first turn and then redirects the team through service lanes once the market proves too crowded.
Takes the alley the moment Sylvanas calls it and kills the rising dead there before they can fold back into the lane.
Uses her horse as moving cover to break the first crush away from the gate and open a lane for Sylvanas and Jaina.
Calls the service-side route immediately instead of letting the trio freeze beneath Arthas’s order.
Takes the grain carter’s directions and converts them into an immediate path to the inner wall.
Once on the wall, shifts from movement support to exit-read duty, keeping fallback lanes in mind while Sylvanas and Jaina witness the purge.
Has the horses, water, and tack ready at the base of the wall before either principal reaches the yard.
Rebuilds the three-rider line around Sylvanas and Jaina without needing the new spacing explained.
Handles the relay handoff and the relay rider's care immediately, turning fresh intelligence into orderly camp motion.
Signals the camp still the instant the friendly bird-call comes, reads the arrival as home trouble rather than enemy pressure, and starts managing blown horses before anyone asks.
Listens from the horse line without intruding on Sylvanas and Vereesa's confrontation, then strikes camp the moment north becomes a spoken order.
Boards the Wolfheart, adapts to shipboard misery without becoming dead weight, and keeps to the useful edge of the formation through the crossing.
Hits Daggercap Bay ready to move inland as soon as Dagny gets them ashore, treating landfall as a continuation of the pursuit rather than an end to the ordeal.
Scouts Muradin’s camp by smoke and line discipline, fights the cave as flank and rear guard, hauls a wounded ranger clear of shardfall, and helps carry Muradin out during the retreat.
Finds the route to Baelgun’s camp through Dragonblight’s broken terrain and then quietly folds Jaina into her protected lane on the return march.
Wipes Jaina’s blood herself on the Wolfheart and keeps close enough during the crossing and road push to catch the next collapse before it becomes a skull crack or a lost pace.
Takes palace positions without being told, widening nearest Jaina once the service arch opens.
Signals the occupied branch and keeps the passage line rather than collapsing the whole team inward before the chamber door.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Accurate border reporting under pressure, including magical anomaly description without overclaiming expertise.
Operational judgment: knows when to escalate, what to secure, and what not to let subordinates mishandle.
Manipulates the ward anchor sequence cleanly enough to drop the holding circle without confusion or delay.
Maintains flank security while a mage is kneeling in the road and the commander is on foot, showing reliable split attention under exposed conditions.
Precise mounted archery under pressure; the skull shot ends a target already moving past mortal injury.
Tracks mass movement through ruined ground well enough to bracket the dead at roughly sixty and identify where spoor becomes real route.
Reads wagon traffic from ruts and weight, distinguishing normal trade in-and-out from civilian flight.
Controls a captured body fast — arm, jaw, balance, transfer — before fear can become sabotage.
Turns to ugly maintenance work without hesitation, handling the physical aftermath other people would waste time recoiling from.
Reads road wear fast enough to tell which loaded teams have been favoring a fork without stopping the march for a formal survey.
Converts watch duty into useful movement data, distinguishing dispatch traffic from patrol pace on the same road.
Turns perimeter scouting into immediate operational guidance, arriving with exactly the traffic and access details the quartet needs to decide whether to leave, press on, or enter quietly.
Turns short-window yard surveillance into targetable intelligence, catching operative behavior through repetition rather than confession or costume.
Performs close-quarters body control in the dark as cleanly as she handles open-road archery.
Maintains rear-guard discipline even while burdened with a corpse, keeping the unit from paying for proof with formation collapse.
Uses clean, economical force under pursuit, answering a threat without generating more chaos than the lane can absorb.
Reads recent wagon scoring, working-road traffic, and estate-approach structure quickly enough to turn scraps into route intelligence.
Prepares horses for fast extraction and keeps flank archery functional through ambush and layered retreat.
Continues operating under fresh grief without losing factual precision.
Reads recent mounted and wagon traffic at a glance and reports only the pieces that change route decisions.
Converts a relay message into immediate movement with no lag between understanding and action.
Can pilot a covert lake crossing close to a warded wall without scraping stone or lighting the watched landing.
Holds a breach and re-entry line alone with enough discipline that the others can use it under pressure on the way back out.
Converts side-lane noise into immediate intercept geometry without waiting for a fuller command phrase.
Keeps the relay chain usable inside emotional and operational pressure, passing messages without adding noise of her own.
Hears and isolates a repeating verbal pattern inside camp noise and motion.
Converts departure logistics into immediate field readiness, from packet handling to lane choice to pace-matching on fresh horses.
Reads urban access points and service-lane congestion quickly enough to reroute a mounted team through a living city without losing minutes.
Maintains flank-kill discipline in cramped urban alleys, dropping rising corpses before they can turn panic into a wider street problem.
Can improvise crowd-and-horse geometry inside urban panic, turning one mounted body into a temporary shield wall.
Can convert civilian route intelligence into immediate tactical movement through unfamiliar service geography.
Can reset horses, tack, water, and formation so quickly that movement resumes before shock hardens into stoppage.
Can absorb a relay at speed and convert it into camp discipline without dropping the line's emotional temperature even further.
Can read a small mounted arrival for urgency, allegiance, and horse-spend before the lead rider dismounts.
Can turn a camp from aftermath posture into departure posture almost on the first syllable of an order.
Can stay operational in an environment outside her normal field domain, enduring a brutal sea crossing without surrendering readiness at the end of it.
Re-anchors the formation quickly at landfall, converting voyage fatigue back into pursuit posture.
Reads defended habitation at distance through smoke, wall line, and posture quickly enough to classify it before first contact.
Maintains rear-guard and casualty-extraction discipline inside a collapsing cave fight without letting the retreat turn ragged.
Extends ordinary Farstrider flank discipline into ad hoc close protection without losing the larger sweep of route, corner, and roofline.
Keeps formation coherence across snow march, shipboard strain, overland sprint, and covert urban entry.
Can communicate corridor threat count and route state cleanly by signal alone while preserving stealth in a live palace interior.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Sylvanas
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses her name directly and expects concise answers.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Lor'themar
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Mentions her by name as a ranger with known judgment.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Vereesa Windrunner
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Receives quiet practical service rather than conversation; chapter 19 treats Cyndia as the competent Farstrider lane beneath Windrunner family pain.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Narration and formation context
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Still treated as the practical ranger edge rather than as a conversational presence, which makes her silent changes in lane assignment more visible.

howSheAddressesOthers.pattern: Road-ready obedience shifts into 'my lady' while keeping the same compressed professionalism.
Refers to her functionally as the detained mage until identity is confirmed from the papers.
Uses 'Mage' as quick escort shorthand once Jaina is back in the saddle and under armed protection.
Chapter 19 returns almost entirely to rank-and-obedience silence; when she does speak, it is in compressed field shorthand rather than personal register.
Chapter 22 leaves the relationship almost entirely in physical support and positioning rather than overt address.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Brief salute, matched stride, and no wasted explanation; she moves like someone who expects to be understood the first time.
Stays at the threshold once the room no longer needs help, showing discipline about when to hold and when to yield space.
Kneels one-handed at the anchor marker, watches the treeline while others talk, and returns to the saddle with the ease of someone for whom vigilance is muscle memory.

In Millhaven she drops to the ground as easily as other people glance over a shoulder — crouched in pens, beside spoor, or over ruts, always with the impression that counting is happening before she speaks.

Chapter 4 keeps her in constant ready motion: spreading just enough to cover the square, clamping Tomas's jaw open before poison is suspected, then turning to boards and nails without a visible emotional detour.

Chapter 5 keeps her in motion on the useful edge of the formation — falling in on Sylvanas's left, scanning the yard while others read paper, and entering the room only long enough to deliver the road report that matters.

Chapter 6 keeps Cyndia in pre-action posture: wider range when the road allows it, a jog back into the group with news already sorted, and weight shifted toward the horses before the final order is spoken aloud.
Chapter 7 keeps her in ready-entry posture: appearing in doorways without sound, bringing the observation before the room finishes forming the question, then going back outside to hold the edge.
Rises from shadow only when the trap closes, then folds back out of focus once the operative is controlled.
Carries the dead operative like spoiled cargo through the first escape turns, making the ugly work look procedural instead of heroic.
Works the practical edge of every scene — horses, left lane, roofline, trench line — until the missing right flank rewrites her habits in real time.
After Lyriel dies, her eyes keep snapping right twice before settling forward again.
Chapter 11 keeps the doubled right-check alive in motion: once on the south road, once again on the northern branch, proof that Lyriel's absence has become embedded drill.
Chapter 11 keeps the doubled right-check alive in motion on both the south road and the final northern branch, proof that Lyriel’s absence has become embedded drill.
Chapter 13 repeats the right-side absence twice in one chapter: once on the shore before the crossing and once at the window before she takes in who has actually come back out.
Keeps checking the empty right out of habit even while the active work stays on the left and center.
Moves first on the relay rider and lowers her bow only after the danger resolves into information.
Holds the left as if the chapter never once needs to explain why that position belongs to her.
At the aide’s blessing her head turns twice in quick succession, the second harder than the first, showing how deeply the repeated phrase has entered her operational awareness.

Chapter 16 keeps her body language pointed outward: knife marking routes, quick returns from corners, and the flat physical confidence of someone who has already assigned herself every piece of work no one else has time to explain.
Gets the horse broadside before anyone finishes thinking through the crush, making her response visibly faster than explanation.
On the wall she takes one look over Sylvanas’s shoulder at the city and says nothing, a silence that reads as comprehension rather than numbness.

Chapter 18 keeps her body language on the useful edge: counting riders on the stair, extending water without fuss, taking the dispatch tube and turning toward camp work before anyone can stall inside the news.

Chapter 19 adds family-pressure witness to that practicality: hand up for stillness at the bird-call, one clean sweep over blown flanks and dispatch tube, then the same left-lane competence once the camp turns north.
At sea she contains discomfort instead of displaying it, staying tucked into necessary work and then resetting visibly once solid ground returns under her.

Chapter 21 keeps her body language in pure field function: hand drifting to the bow at the gate challenge, cutting sideways to haul a ranger out of shardfall, and answering the final body count through exertion rather than commentary.
Keeps Jaina in the corner of her eye like a bad treeline, crouches to wipe blood without ceremony, and repeats the same sweep pattern at the palace arch: door, corner, roofline, Jaina, back again.

knowledge.detail: Knows the mage crossed before dawn, reached the second ring, laid spellwork over the runes, and did not try to flee.
Knows the stones reacted as if something in them answered back rather than as if they had been violently forced.
Confirms identity from the papers: Jaina Proudmoore, apprentice to Antonidas.
Learns the contamination is in ground, water, and living creatures and that Millhaven sits ahead as the first major concentration point.
Confirms there are no bodies in the stream or burn pit, that many villagers likely walked north after death, and that Paret's shed handled multiple wagon visits.
Knows the old trader route north remains populated enough that warning still has live settlements to save.
Sees at close range that the plague operation now includes live village watchers, controlled evidence destruction, and the need for physical prisoner control within seconds of capture.
Knows the south road is carrying both grain teams and Lordaeron troop strings, which makes it more dangerous but also more revealing.
Recognizes the investigation has shifted from village containment to road pursuit, where traffic pattern is now evidence.
Knows the road ahead of Strahnbrad is still moving, with two grain teams half a mile on in the escort churn.
Sees that Andorhal's gate lines are functioning normally and that no one on the approach is checking grain loads at all.
Knows at least one yard worker is checking exits instead of incoming loads and understands that the district is carrying disciplined watchers under labor cover.
Learns the city had search geometry and witness-loss protocol ready before the team ever touched the target.
Knows the unit can still outrun that response when Jaina and Sylvanas are converting warning into movement quickly enough.
Leaves chapter 8 with the field team still intact and turning toward Eastweald while Corin and Havel stay local.
Knows the farmhouse site was fed by recent grain traffic and that the larger route package points toward private estate infrastructure.
Sees a tall hooded figure break the tree line before the crossbows fully react and hears the repeated farewell attached to that retreat.
Carries the three-way estate approach information that lets Jaina take the Barov thread as something more concrete than suspicion.
Knows the Eastweald road is carrying plague quiet rather than battle noise: listless animals, treated-grain stink, and villages orderly enough to prove organized contamination rather than collapse.
Reads Hearthglen's camp approach as active military traffic and confirms the gate lines are moving while no one is checking grain loads.
Receives and acts on the relay that Jaina entered the Barov estate, crossed to Caer Darrow, and had not returned by nightfall.
Knows the Eastweald road is carrying plague quiet rather than battle noise: listless animals, treated-grain stink, and villages orderly enough to prove organized contamination.
Reads Hearthglen’s camp approach as active military traffic and confirms the gate lines are moving while no one is checking grain loads.
Knows the east wall is quieter, the public landing watched, and one upper window left open just enough to matter.
Carries the first external update that Hearthglen is overwhelmed and Arthas is already moving toward Stratholme.
Now knows both Silvermoon and Dalaran have effectively been refused in favor of the southbound mission.
Receives field confirmation that Hearthglen nearly failed again after dark and that Arthas is already racing toward Stratholme.
Knows the team is now running a head-start lane rather than merely chasing relay news behind Arthas.
Carries the fourth confirmed repetition of “May the stars keep your road,” now attached to an ordinary-looking camp aide with a dark ring.

Leaves the chapter with practical approach ideas for Stratholme already in motion: north stores first if they hold, service gate if the market clogs, bread routes if the stock has already been broken up.

Confirms from direct city movement that Stratholme's market, service lanes, loading yards, and bakehouse traffic are still running like ordinary morning commerce even while the plague is already inside them.
Now knows the source run bought two local kills on contaminated stock and still failed to outrun bread already in hands and houses.
Now knows Stratholme’s service-side geography well enough to move from lane to stair under pressure: tallow cut, wheelwright yard, feed crane, inner wall.
Sees the Culling as organized district work rather than uncontrolled panic, even if Sylvanas is the one who fully prices the method below.
Knows the repaired line now functions without the old hesitation: Sylvanas takes point, Jaina settles back into the left-rear lane, and no one has to name the change aloud.
Receives the relay fact that Arthas is taking ships north to Northrend while Uther rides west for Capital City.
Now knows the Convocation has formally recalled Sylvanas under threat of desertion, Vereesa carried the order herself, and Lor'themar bought only a thin mobilization and relay lane to the harbors.
Witnesses both Sylvanas's refusal and Jaina's quieter break from Dalaran before riding north with them.

Learns the sea itself is being pushed against them by the same hostile source pressure Jaina keeps probing, and that Daggercap Bay only proves Arthas is still far ahead rather than near enough to catch.
Now knows Muradin’s camp is the last living foothold on the inland line, the cave fight cannot be won straight, and Muradin is alive enough to justify the carry back to surgery.
Now knows Jaina’s physical state is deteriorating into a standing tactical variable and that the service-lane entry is likely the last quiet threshold before open palace danger.
Now knows the palace interior is compromised deeply enough that even the chamber approach must be handled as silent kill-lane work rather than simple escort.

arcHistory.currentPosition: Established as a reliable border-post ranger whose judgment Sylvanas trusts and whose reports move the plot from rumor to actionable fact.
Still a reliable field professional, but chapter 2 expands her function from border-post witness into active escort and combat-capable investigator support.
Chapter 3 deepens her from flank guard into ground-reader: the ranger Sylvanas trusts most to turn bad dirt into a number and a direction.
Chapter 4 keeps expanding her as the dependable field hand who can make an ugly situation stay physically contained while the leads move on.
Chapter 5 extends Cyndia from ground-tracker and body-control ranger into a moving-road intelligence asset: the woman Sylvanas trusts to tell her what the traffic itself is saying.
Chapter 6 extends Cyndia's role from moving-road intelligence asset into approach scout: the ranger who keeps the quartet's decisions tied to fresh conditions instead of stale argument.
Chapter 7 turns Cyndia from approach scout into city-operation perimeter intelligence: still the dependable ranger on the edge, now feeding the target picture from inside live urban pressure.
Chapter 8 turns Cyndia from perimeter intelligence into full urban rear-guard muscle: the ranger who can do the nastiest physical job in the room and keep the formation moving anyway.

Chapter 11 turns Cyndia from the surviving half of a broken flank into the keeper of that break in live motion. She does not repair the formation; she rides with its ghost and still makes the turn that matters.
Chapter 11 proves the scar tissue holds under pressure. Cyndia carries Lyriel’s absence in drill, not speech, and still pivots cleanly from the Arthas line to the Jaina recovery run.
Chapter 13 keeps Cyndia as the team's surviving exterior discipline. She is still carrying Lyriel's absence, but now she is also the woman who keeps the way back out open and the next road moving.

Chapter 14 keeps Cyndia as the formation's witness and relay carrier. She does not repair the crack between Jaina and Sylvanas; she keeps the damaged line moving fast enough that the crack does not get them all killed.

Chapter 15 keeps Cyndia as the left-hand discipline of the team while quietly upgrading her from relay witness to pattern witness; she is now the first one to catch the fourth farewell clearly enough to matter.

Chapter 16 turns Cyndia from head-start flank support into live urban execution support. She is still the left-hand discipline of the team, but now inside the city itself: route-reader, alley-clearer, and the ranger who keeps the source plan from being swallowed by street confusion.

Chapter 18 extends Cyndia from witness support into recovery support. She is still the practical edge of the formation, but now the work is horses, water, dispatches, and keeping a devastated line operational without asking it to explain itself.

Chapter 20 carries that role across a harsher medium. Cyndia remains the useful edge of the trio even when the road becomes ocean, proving the pursuit still has practical bones once they reach Northrend.
Chapter 21 extends Cyndia from sea-crossing support into defeat-management support. She remains the practical edge that keeps the formation from confusing loss with collapse.

Chapter 22 extends Cyndia from practical support into quiet protective witness. She is still the useful edge of the line, but the line she keeps useful now clearly includes Jaina as part of what must be guarded.

arc.currentPosition: Chapter 23 keeps Cyndia on the outer ring of history-making action: the ranger who does not enter the throne-room core, but whose disciplined hold on the corridor helps make the core possible.
